path.name: AI Software Architect
path.description: A live youth coding program where students learn systems thinking by building and battling autonomous bots in Screeps, a persistent JavaScript strategy world.
path.targetAudience: Students who are ready to build confidence with coding, AI tools, Git, debugging, and real-world problem solving through a structured game-based environment.

programs.name: AI Software Architect Path
programs.description: Students crash-land into Screeps, a living programming world where every worker is controlled by code. Over 3 weeks, they set up a local development environment, learn Git, collaborate with AI, build a working colony bot, and finish with an intra-cohort battle day.
programs.learningOutcomes: Think systematically; describe software behavior in plain language; use AI coding tools responsibly; read and improve generated JavaScript; use Git to store and share code; debug a running system from real feedback.
programs.projectSummary: A local Screeps bot that can harvest energy, spawn workers, upgrade a controller, organize roles, store code in Git, and compete in a final private-server battle day.
programs.portfolioArtifact: A student-owned Git repository with Screeps bot code, learning notes, screenshots, and a final battle-day reflection.
programs.liveSchedule: Tuesdays and Thursdays, 6:30 PM-8:30 PM Eastern
programs.cohortNote: Program week opens Monday, August 3, 2026. Live sessions begin Tuesday, August 4, 2026.

sessions.name: Session 1: Welcome to the World
sessions.objectives: Understand what Screeps is, connect to the local/private server, create the first room, place a spawn, and run the first working creep command.
sessions.liveActivity: Instructor-led setup: Screeps client, local repo, Discord orientation, first room selection, first spawn, first worker.
sessions.homework: Capture one screenshot of your room and write a short note explaining what a spawn, source, creep, and controller do.

sessions.name: Session 2: The First System Loop
sessions.objectives: Build the first harvest-and-return loop and connect game behavior to real-world systems like supply chains and operations.
sessions.liveActivity: Students implement a basic harvester loop, observe errors, and practice describing failures clearly before asking AI for help.
sessions.homework: Let the bot run, log 3 things it does well, and log 3 things that need improvement.

sessions.name: Session 3: Roles, Memory, and Git
sessions.objectives: Introduce role-based architecture, persistent Memory, and Git commits as a way to preserve working progress.
sessions.liveActivity: Create harvester/upgrader/builder roles, initialize or update the program repo, make the first meaningful commits.
sessions.homework: Push code to Git and write a plain-English explanation of each role.

sessions.name: Session 4: Debugging a Living Colony
sessions.objectives: Learn to debug from game state: idle spawns, missing energy, stuck creeps, broken memory, and unclear prompts.
sessions.liveActivity: Students diagnose real bot failures, turn observations into AI prompts, review suggested code, and apply only what they understand.
sessions.homework: Open one issue in the repo describing a bug or improvement in the bot.

sessions.name: Session 5: Battle Prep and Strategy
sessions.objectives: Connect economy, movement, defense, and simple combat strategy to the final battle-day server.
sessions.liveActivity: Prepare a battle-ready branch, discuss simple attack/defense loops, and practice reading opponent behavior as a system.
sessions.homework: Submit the battle branch and write a 5-sentence strategy note.

sessions.name: Session 6: Battle Day
sessions.objectives: Compete in an intra-cohort tournament, observe what strategies hold up, and reflect on systems decisions under pressure.
sessions.liveActivity: Private-server PvP bracket. Students run their bots, watch matches, discuss failures, and document what they would improve next.
sessions.homework: Submit final repo link, screenshots, and battle-day reflection.

offerings.name: AI Software Architect Path - August Cohort
offerings.meetingFrequency: 3 weeks, Tuesdays and Thursdays, 6:30 PM-8:30 PM Eastern

product.title: AutoNateAI Screeps Battle Day
product.cta: The cohort ends with a private-server tournament where students test their bots against classmates.

season.name: August Cohort Battle Day
season.winCondition: Students submit battle-ready bot branches to a private Screeps server. Matches emphasize strategy, debugging, and reflection: what did the bot try to do, what actually happened, and what system would improve next?
